title: Owner Duty, Stakeholder Outcomes, Privacy, and Evidence · Execution Trace: Minimum Data
summary: A team proposes a fast crawler because pages are public but cannot name the site owner, affected users, permitted purpose, stop contact, retained fields, or useful decision. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go minimum data increment in owner-approved site-quality mission charter. Learners progress through prediction, expli…
objectives: Define necessary fields, purpose, access, retention, correction, deletion, aggregation risk, and derived-artifact propagation.
Separate robots preferences and technical access from contracts, privacy, copyright, jurisdiction, and qualified legal review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Minimum Data · Theory Minimum Data
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 7838 during a keyboard-first accessible report review. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate owner permission, stakeholder value, privacy minimization, legal routing, and evidence layers; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define necessary fields, purpose, access, retention, correction, deletion, aggregation risk, and derived-artifact propagation.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-go-minimum-data-i819im(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_crawler_go_minimum_data_i819im\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?charterCases < 10)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?provenCases < 8 \|\| provenCases > charterCases)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?revocationCases < 4)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?authorityGaps > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The decision applies Minimum Data to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Minimum Data.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using necessary and fields plus specific case evidence.
